# FREE Training Matrix Template: Your Guide To Building A Training Matrix

In the next couple of years, 60% of employees will need new training, yet only 41% have started closing their skills gaps. Spotting and addressing these gaps early ensures your team stays skilled, adaptable, resilient, and ready for the future.

Written by Monique Verduyn

Reviewed by Cheryl Marie Tay

8 minutes read

Add as favorite Google source

A conceptual idea of a training matrix template.

As taught in the Learning & Development Certificate Program

4.64 Rating

A training matrix template is necessary for organizations that want to build stronger learning cultures. After all, companies that prioritize learning see 57% higher retention and 23% more internal mobility than those that don’t. With competition for talent intensifying and skills gaps widening, tracking training and development in a structured way offers a strategic edge.

This article discusses how a well-designed template can support this and how to create and maintain a training matrix. It also includes a free, customizable training matrix template you can use to guide your organization’s training and development process.

What is a training matrix? 
How to create and maintain a training matrix

## What is a training matrix?

Atraining matrix is a simple way to track employee learning and development (L&D). It’s usually set up in Excel, Google Sheets, or HR software, with employees listed on one side and required training or certifications across the top. Each cell shows whether training is complete, in progress, or pending, giving managers a clear, detailed overview.

The purpose of a training matrix is visibility. HR Managers, team leads, and Compliance Officers can see who’s on track and who needs support. It highlights employees ready for promotion, prevents duplicate training, and identifies skill gaps. It also acts as proof of compliance in regulated industries, making audits and workforce planning more efficient.

A matrix often includes employee names, training courses, completion dates, and certification expiry dates. Many use color coding (e.g., green for complete, yellow for in progress, red for overdue) for quick insights. HR software can automate the process with reminders for renewals and reports for audits, saving time and reducing errors.

## How to create and maintain a training matrix

HR is central to building an effective employee training matrix. Here’s how to develop and implement an efficient training matrix that meets both business and workforce needs:

### Design the structure

Take the lead in setting up the training matrix framework. This means deciding which training programs, certifications, and skill areas to track, and choosing how to display the information. A clear, user-friendly design makes it easy for managers and team leads to see who has completed what and what’s still pending.

### Gather requirements and update progress

Work with department heads to define training needs for different roles. Once the matrix is up and running, coordinate with managers to keep it current, adding new training programs, recording completions, and updating certification renewals.

### Align with company goals and compliance

Ensure the company’s training matrix reflects its bigger objectives, whether they involve preparing employees for leadership, strengthening digital skills, or meeting industry compliance standards. In regulated environments, the matrix also proves during audits that employees have received proper training.

### Spot gaps and set priorities

By reviewing the matrix regularly, you can spot patterns like departments missing key skills, teams that excel, or individuals ready for the next level of development. This makes setting priorities, allocating resources, and ensuring the workforce has the right capabilities easier.

### **Learn to develop a training matrix to boost workforce success**

To build a training matrix that drives workforce success, you must ensure it reflects company needs, align staff skills with business goals, and define required competencies.

## 10 training matrix examples

Below are 10 examples of training matrices organizations may use for different purposes, from employee onboarding to succession planning.

1. **Onboarding matrix:** Tracks all the training new hires must complete when they join the company, such as orientation, role-specific processes, compliance briefings and safety procedures.
2. **Cross-training matrix:** Shows which employees are capable of stepping into other roles when colleagues are absent, making it easy to manage coverage during vacations, illness or peak workloads.
3. **Compliance training matrix:** Keeps track of mandatory courses like workplace safety, data protection or industry-specific certifications, ensuring the company is always audit-ready.
4. **Leadership development matrix:** Maps out training for employees on a leadership track, from soft skills (e.g., communication and conflict resolution) to advanced management courses and mentoring.
5. **Technical skills matrix:** Focuses on digital or job-specific technical skills (e.g., coding languages or software certifications), helping managers see where expertise is concentrated and where upskilling is needed.
6. **Project-based training matrix:** Used in project teams to ensure members have the right mix of skills for a specific project. These might include agile training, project management certifications, or specialist tools training.
7. **Customer service training matrix:** Covers training in areas like customer interaction, product knowledge, complaint handling, and sales techniques, making sure front-line employees deliver consistent service.
8. **Sales training matrix:** This matrix tracks skills such as negotiation, CRM system use, product demonstrations**,** and closing techniques, helping identify top performers and development opportunities.
9. **Remote work skills matrix:** A newer type of matrix that ensures employees working remotely are trained in collaboration tools, cybersecurity, digital communication, and time management.
10. **Succession planning matrix:** Aligns with long-term strategic workforce planning by tracking skills of potential successors for key roles, making sure they receive the right training and development opportunities ahead of time.

## Why use a training matrix template?

Whether you build your own or download a free training matrix template, there are some compelling reasons to use one:

### Clear overview of team capabilities

An employee training matrix gives you a visual snapshot of who has what skills, certifications, or completed training. Instead of digging through files or asking around, you can see at a glance how prepared each team is and which areas they may need more support in.

### Easy tracking of training status

A training matrix template can help simplify the process of updating records as employees complete training. You can quickly check who has completed their training, whose training is still in progress, and who still needs to get started. This makes reporting much easier.

### Supports succession planning and promotions

A training matrix highlights employees who are ready for the next step in their careers. Showcasing training and development progress helps HR and managers identify future leaders and suitable candidates for internal promotions.

### Identifies cross-training opportunities

Using a training matrix makes it easy to spot where skills overlap and where there are gaps. This helps managers create cross-training plans, so employees can cover one another during absences or take on new responsibilities whenever necessary.

### Reduces compliance risks

In industries with strict regulations, an up-to-date training matrix ensures that no one in the organization misses mandatory training and certifications. This reduces the risk of compliance failures and potential legal problems and provides proof of completion during audits.

### Saves time compared to manual tracking

Instead of juggling individual files or spreadsheets, a training matrix template centralizes everything in one place. This means less time spent manually updating records and more time focusing on more strategic activities, like succession planning and employee development.

[]() 

## Key sections of a training matrix template

Here’s what every employee training matrix template must include:

* **Employee names/IDs and roles:** Each column represents an individual employee — listed by name (or employee ID) and designation — to minimize confusion about whose training record is being tracked.
* **Skills, certifications, and training needs:** The columns capture the specific skills and certifications essential for each role or department. These typically fall into three categories: technical and soft skills, proficiency levels, and role-specific vs. company-wide training.
* **Status indicators:** A good template clearly shows training progress. Simple symbols or checkboxes work, but many companies use traffic light color coding for instant visibility.
* **Dates of completion or expiry:** Tracking dates is critical for certifications that require renewal, such as safety or compliance training. Expiry dates make it easy to plan refresher courses before credentials lapse.
* **Responsible manager or trainer:** Including the name of the manager or trainer accountable for each program creates accountability and ensures a clear point of contact for follow-up.
* **Data management:** This helps organize data so HR and managers can act on it. This includes capturing completion status, renewal dates, and assessment scores, flagging expired compliance-related training, and displaying progress through dashboards, percentage completion, or color coding.

## 7 types of training matrix templates and when to use them

Whether you choose a free training matrix template or create your own, the right option depends on your goals. Here are some common types and how you can use them effectively:

### 1\. Basic employee training matrix template

If you’re running a small business or managing a team that’s just starting to track training formally, use a basic employee training matrix template. It lets you keep track of employees’ training progress and status, giving you a simple, no-frills way to see who’s on track and who needs more support.

### 2\. Cross-training matrix template

A cross-training matrix template helps build team flexibility by showing who can step into different roles. This is especially useful when planning for vacations, covering absences, or preparing for peak workloads. This template can help reduce disruption and keep work moving even when key people are away.

### 3\. Role-specific training matrix template

If your teams have very different responsibilities, you’ll want a role-specific training matrix template that can connect training to career paths. It lists the training requirements for each job title or department, so you can make sure employees are gaining the skills they need to fulfill role expectations.

Infographic listing 7 types of training matrix templates and when HR should use each one.

### 4\. Compliance training matrix template

A compliance training matrix is essential if your industry requires mandatory courses such as safety, data protection, or regulatory certifications. Use this template to make sure nothing slips through the cracks, so your organization can stay audit-ready and avoid the legal risks that come with expired or missing certifications.

### 5\. Leadership development training matrix template

If you’re preparing employees to step into management or leadership positions, use this template to track leadership skills such as communication, conflict resolution, and strategic decision-making. This template is also a practical tool for monitoring leadership skills to support succession planning.

### 6\. Technical skills training matrix template

When your business relies on specialist expertise, use a technical skills training matrix to track technical certifications, software knowledge, or equipment training. This makes it easier to spot which areas you need to upskill your team in and whether you need to bring in additional expertise.

### 7\. Remote work training matrix template

If your employees work remotely, you can use this template to make sure they’re trained in collaboration tools, cybersecurity, time management, and virtual communication. It helps you build a productive, secure, and well-connected workforce, no matter where in the world they’re based.

[]() 

### To sum up

An employee training matrix provides a structured approach to tracking skills, certifications, and training progress, allowing your workforce to adapt quickly to new challenges. A training matrix template gives you a bird’s-eye view of team strengths, hidden gaps, and opportunities for growth. That visibility makes training a driver of long-term capability.

A training matrix also assures staff that their development matters. Those who can see a clear path of learning and advancement are more likely to feel engaged and invested in their roles. Whatever your business size or objectives, the right training matrix template will help your company build resilience, reduce risk, and foster a culture of continuous improvement.
